assessment_items.question_content:
**The facade of the Pantheon looks like an oversized Greek temple.  All of the following contribute to this visual effect, EXCEPT the . . .**


![The Pantheon, Rome, c. 125 C.E.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/06cfb98b8942c8e01f561197dade8189.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The facade of the building looks like an oversized Greek temple, with a large porch, columns, and pediment.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Pantheon was built to contain a perfect sphere and was influenced by Greek mathematical ideas.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Greek mathematical ideas was important influence on the construction of the Pantheon.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The current building was built under the direction of the Emperor Augustus and it replaced an earlier Pantheon built by Caesar.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The current building was built under the direction of Emperor Hadrian and it replaced an earlier Pantheon by Agrippa. **

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the Pantheon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The dome of the Pantheon was made of concrete.**

choices.content: When it was made, the Pantheon was the largest dome in the world and it would be hundreds of years before architects built another dome of a similar size.
The Pantheon is a temple and was dedicated to the seven main gods in the Roman religion.
When Rome was sacked by Germanic tribes in the fifth century, they removed the gold that decorated the Pantheon.
The dome of the Pantheon was constructed with precisely cut marble bricks.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Please match the architectural feature with the appropriate image from the Pantheon.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: The oculus is the circle in the center of the dome that lets in light. Revetment refers to the colored marble facing. The coffers are the decorative recessed panels that decorate the dome. A corinthian capital is the top of a column carved with acanthus leaves.

right: ![Round, open window at the top of the dome, The Pantheon, Rome, c. 125 C.E.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/20202a28525d7f83b10e377bcbe35a67.png)
![Ornamental carving at the top of a column, The Pantheon, Rome, c. 125 C.E.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/07418f1a3646ffff5703e1054f1ca7d3.jpg)
![Square inset panel on the interior of the dome, The Pantheon, Rome, c. 125 C.E.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d37c6bfbfbddb42c617823d2d425415.png)
![Stone wall, The Pantheon, Rome, c. 125 C.E.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/986b489e33c3b8ffc557e6f3644316c1.jpg)
